value_description: Alcohol by VolumeABV is the most common measurement of alcohol content in beer; it simply indicates how much of the total volume of liquid in a beer is made up of alcohol.

IBU stands for International Bitterness Units, a scale to gauge the level of a beer's bitterness. More specifically, IBUs measure the parts per million of is humulone from hops in a beer, which gives beer bitterness.
